title: Drap identification and decoding
abstract: The proposed technology comprises identifying and decoding a dependent random access point, DRAP, sample and its associated intra random access point, IRAP, sample in a media container file (1 ) comprising a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures. The proposed solution also introduces DRAP functionality to media container files e.g. by including information identifying a position of the DRAP sample and including information in a media container file which enables identification of a closest preceding IRAP sample within a bitstream and said closest preceding IRAP sample is a reference sample for the DRAP sample.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for decoding dependent random access point, DRAP, samples, said method comprising: finding (S2) a DRAP sample in a media container file (1) comprising a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures; finding (S3) a closest preceding, according to a decoding order, intra random access point, IRAP, sample in said media container file (1); decoding (S4) said IRAP sample; and decoding (S5) said DRAP sample with said decoded IRAP sample as reference sample. 2. The method according to claim 1 , further comprising receiving (S1) a tune-in or seek request defining a desired tune-in or seek position within said media container file (1), wherein finding (S2) said DRAP sample comprises finding (S2) a DRAP sample in said media container file (1) associated with said desired tune-in or seek position based on said tune-in or seek request. 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein finding (S2) said associated DRAP sample comprises finding (S2) a closest preceding, according to said decoding order and relative to said tune-in or seek position, DRAP sample in said media container file (1) based on said tune-in or seek request. 4. The method according to any of the claims 1 to 3, wherein finding (S2) said DRAP sample comprises inspecting (S2) a DRAP sample group (7) in said media container file (1) to find said DRAP sample; and finding (S3) said closest preceding IRAP sample comprises finding and extracting (S3) an immediately preceding IRAP sample from said media container file (1). 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein finding and extracting (S3) said immediately preceding IRAP sample comprises inspecting (S3) a random access point, RAP, sample group (7) in said media container file (1) in order to retrieve information defining a position of said IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 6. The method according to claim 4, wherein finding and extracting (S3) said immediately preceding IRAP sample comprises inspecting (S3) a sync sample table box (8) in said media container file (1) in order to retrieve information defining a position of said IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 7. The method according to claim 4, wherein finding and extracting (S3) said immediately preceding IRAP sample comprises inspecting (S3) a current or previous track fragment run box in order to retrieve information defining a position of said IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 5 8. The method according to any of the claims 1 to 3, wherein finding (S2) said DRAP sample comprises: inspecting a list comprising information of sample type and/or sample property information; and identifying said DRAP sample as a sample indicated as DRAP sample in said list and that 10 is closest to a desired tune-in or seek position; and finding (S3) said IRAP sample comprises inspecting (S3) said list to identify a sample indicated as IRAP sample in said list and that is closest to and preceding said identified DRAP sample. 9. A method of generating a media container file (1), said method comprising: 15 including (S10) a sample group description (6) and/or a sample group (7) with grouping type 'drap' comprising information identifying a position of a dependent random access point, DRAP, sample within a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures comprised in said media container file (1); including (S11) information in said media container file (1) of a closest preceding intra random 20 access point, IRAP, sample within said bitstream given a position of said DRAP sample in said bitstream, said information enables identification of said closest preceding IRAP sample within said bitstream and said closest preceding IRAP sample is a reference sample for said DRAP sample; and including (S12) said bitstream in said media container file (1). 25 10. The method according to claim 9, wherein including (S11) said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample comprises including (S11) a sync sample table box (8) comprising said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 11. The method according to claim 9, wherein including (S11) said information of said closest 30 preceding IRAP sample comprises including (S11), in said media container file (1), a sample group description (6) and/or a sample group (7) with grouping type 'rap ' comprising said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample. 12. The method according to claim 9, wherein said media container file (1) is fragmented into multiple file fragments and including (S11) said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample comprises including (S11) a track fragment run box comprising said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample in a file fragment of said multiple file fragments. 13. The method according to claim 12, wherein including (S11) said track fragment run box comprises including (S11) said track fragment run box in a same file fragment of said multiple file fragments as said sample group description and sample group with grouping type 'drap' or in a previous file fragment of said multiple file fragments. 14. A media container file (1 ) comprising: a movie box (2) comprising metadata of a presentation; at least one media data box (3) comprising a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures; a sample table box (5) comprising metadata related to samples in said media container file (1), wherein said sample table box (5) comprises: a sample group description box (6) with grouping type equal to 'drap'; and a sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'drap', wherein said sample group description box (6) with grouping type equal to 'drap' and said sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'drap' comprise information allowing identification of one or more dependent random access point, DRAP, samples in said bitstream contained in said at least one media data box (3); information allowing identification of a closest preceding intra random access point, IRAP, sample in said bitstream given a position of a DRAP sample in said bitstream, wherein said position of said DRAP sample in said bitstream is defined based on said information comprised in said sample group description box (6) with grouping type equal to 'drap' and said sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'drap'. 15. The media container file according to claim 14, further comprising: a sample group description box (6) with grouping type equal to 'rap '; and a sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'rap ', wherein said sample group description box (6) with grouping type equal to 'rap ' and said sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'rap ' comprise information allowing identification of IRAP samples in said bitstream contained in said at least one media data box (3), and 

 said information comprised in said sample group description box (6) with grouping type equal to 'rap ' and said sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'rap ' defines a closest preceding IRAP sample in said bitstream, given said position of said DRAP sample. 16. The media container file according to claim 15, wherein a closest preceding sample, relative to said position of said DRAP sample, in said sample group box (7) with grouping type equal to 'rap ' is marked as an IRAP picture in said bitstream. 17. The media container file according to claim 14, further comprising a sync sample table box (8) comprising information enabling identification of said closest preceding IRAP sample in the bitstream given said position of said DRAP sample. 18. The media container file according to claim 17, wherein a closest preceding sample, relative to said position of said DRAP sample, in said sync sample table box (8) is marked as an IRAP picture in said bitstream. 19. The media container file according to any of the claims 14 to 18, wherein said media container file (1) is fragmented into multiple file fragments, each file fragment of said multiple file fragments comprises: a media data box comprising a part of said encoded video content of said bitstream; a movie fragment box comprising metadata relating to said part of said encoded video content comprised in said media data box of said file fragment, said move fragment box comprises a track fragment run box comprising information enabling identification of positions of IRAP samples in said bitstream. 20. The media container file according to claim 19, wherein said information comprised in said track fragment box is in the form of a flag indicating whether an associated sample is an IRAP sample or not. 21. A device (100, 110) for decoding dependent random access point, DRAP, samples, wherein said device (100, 110) is configured to find a DRAP sample in a media container file (1) comprising a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures; said device (100, 110) is configured to find a closest preceding, according to a decoding order, intra random access point, IRAP, sample in said media container file (1); said device (100, 110) is configured to decode said IRAP sample; and 

 said device (100, 110) is configured to decode said DRAP sample with said decoded IRAP sample as reference sample. 22. The device according to claim 21 , wherein 5 said device (100, 110) is configured to receive a tune-in or seek request defining a desired tune- in or seek position within said media container file (1 ); and said device (100, 110) is configured to find a DRAP sample in said media container file (1) associated with said desired tune-in or seek position based on said tune-in or seek request. 10 23. The device according to claim 22, wherein said device (100, 110) is configured to find a closest preceding, according to said decoding order and relative to said tune-in or seek position, DRAP sample in said media container file (1) based on said tune-in or seek request. 24. The device according to any of the claims 21 to 23, wherein 15 said device (100, 110) is configured to inspect a DRAP sample group (7) in said media container file (1) to find said DRAP sample; and said device (100, 110) is configured to find and extract an immediately preceding IRAP sample from said media container file (1). 20 25. The device according to claim 24, wherein said device (100, 110) is configured to inspect a random access point, RAP, sample group (7) in said media container file (1) in order to retrieve information defining a position of said IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 26. The device according to claim 24, wherein said device (100, 110) is configured to inspect a sync 25 sample table box (8) in said media container file (1) in order to retrieve information defining a position of said IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 27. The device according to claim 24, wherein said device (100, 110) is configured to inspect a current or previous track fragment run box in order to retrieve information defining a position of said 30 IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 28. The device according to any of the claims 21 to 23, wherein said device (100, 110) is configured to inspect a list comprising information of sample type and/or sample property information; 

 said device (100, 110) is configured to identify said DRAP sample as a sample indicated as DRAP said device (100, 110) is configured to inspect said list to identify a sample indicated as IRAP sample in said list and that is closest to and preceding said identified DRAP sample. 29. The device according to any of the claims 21 to 28, further comprising: a processor (111); and a memory (112) comprising instructions executable by said processor (111), wherein said processor (111 ) is operative to find said DRAP sample in said media container file (1 ); said processor (111) is operative to find said IRAP sample in said media container file (!); said processor (111) is operative to decode said IRAP sample; and said processor (111) is operative to decode said DRAP sample. 30. A device (120) for decoding dependent random access point, DRAP, samples, said device (130) comprises a DRAP finder (121) for finding a DRAP sample in a media container file (1) comprising a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures; an intra random access point, IRAP, finder (122) for finding a closest preceding, according to a decoding order, IRAP sample in said media container file (1); and a video decoder (123) for decoding said IRAP sample and decoding said DRAP sample with said decoded IRAP sample as reference sample. 31. A device (200) for generating a media container file (1 ), wherein said device (200) is configured to include a sample group description (6) and/or a sample group (7) with grouping type 'drap' comprising information identifying a position of a dependent random access point, DRAP, sample within a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures comprised in said media container file (1); said device (200) is configured to include information in said media container file (1) of a closest preceding intra random access point, IRAP, sample within said bitstream given a position of said DRAP sample in said bitstream, said information enables identification of said closest preceding IRAP sample within said bitstream and said closest preceding IRAP sample is a reference sample for said DRAP sample; and said device (200) is configured to include said bitstream in said media container file (1). 32. The device according to claim 31 , wherein said device (200) is configured to include a sync sample table box (8) comprising said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample in said media container file (1). 33. The device according to claim 31 , wherein said device (200) is configured to include, in said media container file (1), a sample group description (6) and/or sample group (7) with grouping type 'rap ' comprising said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample. 34. The device according to claim 31 , wherein said media container file (1) is fragmented into multiple file fragments and said device (200) is configured to include a track fragment run box comprising said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample in a file fragment of said multiple file fragments. 35. The device according to claim 34, wherein said device (200) is configured to include said track fragment run box in a same file fragment of said multiple file fragments as said sample group description and sample group with grouping type 'drap' or in a previous file fragment of said multiple file fragments. 36. The device according to any of the claims 31 to 35, further comprising: a processor (201); and a memory (202) comprising instructions executable by said processor (201), wherein said processor (201) is operative to include said sample group description (6) and/or said sample group (7) with grouping type drag in said media container file (1); said processor (201) is operative to include said information of said closest preceding IRAP sample in said media container file (1); and said processor (201) is operative to include said bitstream in said media container file (1). 37. A device (210) for generating a media container file (1 ), said device (210) comprises: a group including unit (211) for including a sample group description (6) and/or a sample group (7) with grouping type 'drap' comprising information identifying a position of a dependent random access point, DRAP, sample within a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures comprised in said media container file (1); an information including unit (212) for including information in said media container file (1) of a closest preceding intra random access point, IRAP, sample within said bitstream given a position of 

 said DRAP sample in said bitstream, said information enables identification of said closest preceding IRAP sample within said bitstream and said closest preceding IRAP sample is a reference sample for said DRAP sample; and a bitstream including unit (213) for including said bitstream in said media container file (1). 5 38. A computer program (340) comprising instructions, which when executed by a processor (310), cause said processor (310) to: find a dependent random access point, DRAP, sample in a media container file (1) comprising a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures; 10 find a closest preceding, according to a decoding order, intra random access point, IRAP, sample in said media container file (1); decode said IRAP sample; and decode said DRAP sample with said decoded IRAP sample as reference sample. 15 39. A computer program (340) comprising instructions, which when executed by a processor (310), cause said processor (310) to: include a sample group description (6) and/or a sample group (7) with grouping type 'drap' comprising information identifying a position of a dependent random access point, DRAP, sample within a bitstream of encoded video content in the form of encoded pictures comprised in said media 20 container file (1); include information in said media container file (1) of a closest preceding intra random access point, IRAP, sample within said bitstream given a position of said DRAP sample in said bitstream, said information enables identification of said closest preceding IRAP sample within said bitstream and said closest preceding IRAP sample is a reference sample for said DRAP sample; and 25 include said bitstream in said media container file (1). 40. A carrier (350) comprising a computer program (340) according to claim 38 or 39, wherein said carrier (350) is one of an electronic signal, an optical signal, an electromagnetic signal, a magnetic signal, an electric signal, a radio signal, a microwave signal, or a computer-readable storage medium. 30
